Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1946 upvotes, 254 comments
  2. Where Some See Strings, She Sees a Space-Time Made of Fractals 37 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Show HN: I built an ISP infrastructure emulator from scratch with a custom vBNG 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 431 upvotes, 165 comments
  5. Whistleblower claims ex-DOGE member says he took Social Security data to new job 478 upvotes, 191 comments
  6. AI Agent Hacks McKinsey 200 upvotes, 78 comments
  7. Temporal: A nine-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 140 upvotes, 52 comments
  8. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 177 upvotes, 69 comments
  9. PeppyOS: A simpler alternative to ROS 2 (now with containers support) 56 upvotes, 18 comments
  10. Entities enabling scientific fraud at scale are large, resilient, growing (2025) 170 upvotes, 73 comments
  11. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 89 upvotes, 34 comments
  12. Faster asin() was hiding in plain sight 110 upvotes, 46 comments
  13. Bypassing PatchGuard on Windows x64 (2005) 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  14. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 358 upvotes, 197 comments
  15. BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 200 upvotes, 106 comments
  16. Writing my own text editor, and daily-driving it 184 upvotes, 98 comments
  17. Elevated errors on login with Claude Code 37 upvotes, 18 comments
  18. Visualizing Ukkonen's Suffix Tree Algorithm 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  19. Show HN: Open-source browser for AI agents 26 upvotes, 12 comments
  20. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 569 upvotes, 462 comments
  21. Lego's 0.002mm specification and its implications for manufacturing (2025) 262 upvotes, 206 comments
  22. Launch HN: Prism (YC X25) – Workspace and API to generate and edit videos 14 upvotes, 5 comments
  23. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 30 upvotes, 16 comments
  24. Show HN: Vanilla JavaScript refinery simulator built to explain job to my kids 18 upvotes, 8 comments
  25. Wiz joins Google 72 upvotes, 51 comments
  26. Fungal Electronics 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  27. Launch HN: Sentrial (YC W26) – Catch AI Agent Failures Before Your Users Do 8 upvotes, 5 comments
  28. Why the global elite gave up on spelling and grammar 35 upvotes, 79 comments
  29. Swiss e-voting pilot can't count 2,048 ballots after decryption failure 29 upvotes, 73 comments
  30. The MacBook Neo 29 upvotes, 131 comments

Last generated 1:30am, 12 March 2026. Source here.
